message:
I'll be honest—I'm not the 'prepare for everything' type. But last winter, I finally did one tiny thing I'd been putting off: I made a simple list of what to grab if we had to leave fast (meds, docs, phone chargers, the kids' comfort items). Stuck it on the fridge.

Then we got that surprise ice storm that knocked out power for two days. Power was fine at my sister's place across town, so we packed up. And you know what? Grabbing that list took maybe 30 seconds instead of the 20 minutes of "Wait, did I forget anything?" stress.

It's wild how one small decision—just *writing something down*—can turn panic into "okay, we got this."

Have you ever made a teeny-tiny prep move that actually made a difference when you needed it? I'd love to hear what it was. 💙
